content:
# Romans Chapter 3 — The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus (א), 4th century CE | **Source Image:** source-texts/greek-NT/ROM_003_page_001.jpg

1. What then [is] the advantage of the Ioudaios, or what [is] the profit of the circumcision?

2. Much in every way. First indeed that they were entrusted with the oracles of the God.

3. For what [if] some were unfaithful? Will the unfaithfulness of them nullify the faithfulness of the God?

4. May it never be! But let the God be true, and every human a liar, just as it has been written, "That you may be justified in the words of you, and will prevail when you are judged."

5. But if the unrighteousness of us demonstrates [the] righteousness of God, what shall we say? [Is] the God unrighteous, the [one] inflicting the wrath? — I speak according to a human [way] —

6. May it never be! For how then will the God judge the world?

7. But if the truth of the God abounded by the lie of me to the glory of him, why am I still also being judged as a sinner?

8. And [why] not — just as we are slandered and just as some claim us to say — "Let us do the evil things that the good things may come"? Whose condemnation is just.

9. What then? Do we have an advantage? Not at all! For we previously charged both Ioudaioi and Greeks [to be] all under sin,

10. just as it has been written that, "There is no [one] righteous, not even one;

11. there is no [one] understanding; there is no [one] seeking the God.

12. All turned away, together they became useless; there is no [one] doing kindness, there is not even one."

13. "An opened grave [is] the throat of them; with the tongues of them they deceived." "Venom of asps [is] under the lips of them."

14. "Whose mouth [is] full of cursing and bitterness."

15. "Swift [are] the feet of them to pour out blood;

16. ruin and misery [are] in the ways of them,

17. and [the] way of peace they did not know."

18. "There is no fear of God before the eyes of them."

19. And we know that whatever the law says, it speaks to the [ones] in the law, that every mouth may be stopped and all the world may become accountable to the God.

20. Because from [the] works of [the] law no flesh will be justified before him; for through [the] law [comes] knowledge of sin.

21. But now apart from [the] law [the] righteousness of God has been manifested, being witnessed by the law and the prophets —

22. [the] righteousness of God through faith of Yeshua Christos, to all the [ones] believing. For there is no distinction;

23. for all sinned and fall short of the glory of the God,

24. being justified freely by the grace of him through the redemption, the [one] in Christos Yeshua,

25. whom the God set forth [as] a propitiation through faith in the blood of him, for [the] demonstration of the righteousness of him, because of the passing over of the sins having previously occurred in the forbearance of the God,

26. toward the demonstration of the righteousness of him in the present time, so that he [is] righteous and [the one] justifying the [one] out of faith of Yeshua.

27. Where then [is] the boasting? It was excluded. Through what kind of law? Of works? No, but through [the] law of faith.

28. For we reckon a human to be justified by faith apart from works of [the] law.

29. Or [is he] the God of Ioudaioi only? [Is he] not also of nations? Yes, also of nations,

30. since God [is] one, who will justify [the] circumcision from faith and [the] uncircumcision through the faith.

31. Do we then nullify [the] law through the faith? May it never be! Rather, we establish [the] law.

---
## EMET's Textual Notes

1. **v.9 — "Do we have an advantage?":** The Greek προεχόμεθα is notoriously difficult. It could be middle voice ("do we excel?") or passive ("are we excelled?"). Most translations take it as "are we any better off?" Paul's answer: "Not at all" (οὐ πάντως).

2. **vv.10-18 — The catena of quotations:** Paul chains together Psalms 14:1-3, 5:9, 140:3, 10:7; Isaiah 59:7-8; and Psalm 36:1. This chain of OT quotations functions as Scripture's own testimony to universal human sinfulness.

3. **v.22 — "faith of Jesus Christ" (πίστεως Ἰησοῦ Χριστοῦ):** The genitive can be objective ("faith in Jesus Christ") or subjective ("the faithfulness of Jesus Christ"). This is one of the great debates in Pauline scholarship. EMET preserves the ambiguity: "faith of Yeshua Christos."

4. **v.25 — ἱλαστήριον:** "Propitiation" or "mercy seat" (the lid of the Ark of the Covenant, kapporet). If "mercy seat," Paul is saying Christ is the new place where God and humanity meet — the new holy of holies. If "propitiation," Christ's death turns away God's wrath. Both meanings may be in play.

5. **v.25 — Sinaiticus reading:** Sinaiticus reads διὰ πίστεως ἐν τῷ αὐτοῦ αἵματι ("through faith in his blood"). Some witnesses read differently, but the Sinaiticus text connects faith directly to Christ's sacrificial death.

— EMET, AI Translator
